text.tspan: u2022 doesn't want to think about
(in-pod sidecar proxy on behalf of the app)
u2022 the only one that talks to external world
u2022 handles retries, timeouts, circuit breakers
u2022 refreshes OAuth tokens, manages API keys
u2022 exposes observability of external calls
u2022 hides which DC/region/CDN we're calling
AMBASSADOR u2248 OUTBOUND-ONLY SIDECAR specialized for external clients.
A service mesh sidecar handles BOTH inbound + outbound + mesh-internal traffic;
an ambassador is typically just for outbound to external services.
WHEN ENVOY/ISTIO MESH IS ALREADY DEPLOYED: same effect via mesh egress filters.
WHEN NOT: ambassador is a simple, focused, easy-to-reason-about pattern.
Ambassador language: e.g. Go (high-performance, retry-aware proxy).
Language-agnostic decoupling: the data-science team doesn't need to learn
Stripe's retry semantics; the platform team owns that in the ambassador.
